summary: Matched Dolphin OSContext and OSInterrupt work by converting large raw asm/data fragments into typed C/inline-asm sources, moving formerly separate data objects into C, and tightening shared headers for interrupt masks, context layout, hardware-register externs, and volatile interrupt state.
what_changed: Deleted asm/dolphin/os/OSContext_data.s and asm/dolphin/os/OSInterrupt_data.s, then removed their object entries from obj_files.mk, indicating their data was absorbed into C sources.

Reworked OSContext.c from mostly raw inline assembly with address comments into typed MWERKS_GEKKO inline asm plus C-visible logic. Examples include __OSLoadFPUContext, __OSSaveFPUContext, OSLoadFPUContext, OSSaveFPUContext, OSSetCurrentContext, OSSaveContext, OSLoadContext, OSInitContext, OSDumpContext, OSSwitchFPUContext, and __OSContextInit.
OSContext.h renamed context fields SHORT_0x1A0 and SHORT_0x1A2 to mode and state, removed an explicit padding member before psfs, and gave OSInitContext typed u32 pc/newsp parameters.

OSInterrupt.c gained C data and C logic for InterruptHandlerTable, InterruptPrioTable, volatile __OSLastInterruptTime/__OSLastInterrupt/__OSLastInterruptSrr0, __OSInterruptInit, SetInterruptMask, __OSMaskInterrupts, __OSUnmaskInterrupts, __OSDispatchInterrupt, and ExternalInterruptHandler.

OSInterrupt.h introduced typedef u32 OSInterruptMask and many OS_INTRMASK_* macros built as 0x80000000U >> OS_INTR_* values, including grouped masks such as OS_INTRMASK_MEM, OS_INTRMASK_DSP, OS_INTRMASK_EXI, OS_INTRMASK_PI, and OS_INTRMASK_PI_PE.
OSInterrupt.h changed last-interrupt globals to volatile externs and declared __RAS_OSDisableInterrupts_begin/end labels used by OSDisableInterrupts inline asm.
Moved shared hardware-register extern declarations into headers: __MEMRegs moved to OSMemory.h and __EXIRegs plus EXI_REG_MAX moved to OSExi.h.
DBPrintf was made const-correct and declared in db.h as void DBPrintf(const char* str, ...).
OSError.c references were adjusted around __OSLastInterruptTime and lbl_804D738C, including replacing an asm comment reference to lbl_804D738C with __OSLastInterruptTime+4.
smart_moves: Converted anonymous data labels for context dump strings and interrupt priority data into named C uses, enabling deletion of separate data .s objects and simplifying obj_files.mk.

Kept fragile CPU state save/restore routines as MWERKS_GEKKO inline asm while replacing raw numeric struct accesses where possible with typed OSContext fields such as fpuctx->state, fpuctx->fpscr, and fpuctx->fprs[i].
Used OSInterruptMask and enum-derived OS_INTRMASK_* macros to make interrupt bit manipulation readable while preserving the original high-bit mask convention.
Centralized hardware register arrays in headers so OSInterrupt.c could share __MEMRegs and __EXIRegs without duplicating local extern declarations.
Marked last-interrupt globals volatile, matching their interrupt-side update semantics and avoiding misleading ordinary-data declarations.
Preserved RAS/profiling entry labels for OSDisableInterrupts by naming __RAS_OSDisableInterrupts_begin and __RAS_OSDisableInterrupts_end rather than leaving anonymous labels.
decomp_lessons: For low-level Dolphin OS code, matching often benefits from a hybrid style: keep true context-switch and MSR/FPU operations in inline asm, but give the asm typed signatures and struct-field references where the compiler accepts them.

When data-only asm files contain strings, priority tables, or sbss globals that can be represented naturally in C, migrate them into the owning C file and remove their object files from the build to reduce artificial split points.
Interrupt masks should be named as masks, not addresses. The reviewed 0x80000000U base literal is a mask seed shifted by interrupt enum value, not a virtual-address constant.
Use volatile for globals written/read across interrupt paths, hardware state, or diagnostic paths; reviewers explicitly discussed that standard volatile semantics were sufficient here.
Expose shared MMIO register arrays through headers when multiple OS subsystems need them, instead of repeating file-local externs that can drift in size or naming.
Const-correct variadic debug functions matter for matching callers that pass string literals; DBPrintf changed from char* to const char*.
naming_conventions: Interrupt mask constants use OS_INTRMASK_<subsystem>_<source>, derived from matching OS_INTR_<subsystem>_<source> enum names.
Grouped interrupt masks use subsystem-wide names such as OS_INTRMASK_MEM, OS_INTRMASK_AI, OS_INTRMASK_DSP, OS_INTRMASK_EXI, OS_INTRMASK_PI, and OS_INTRMASK_PI_PE.
Raw context fields were promoted from placeholder names SHORT_0x1A0/SHORT_0x1A2 to semantic names mode and state.
External interrupt handler naming replaced an address-style placeholder lbl_80347B80 with ExternalInterruptHandler.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO around inline asm implementations with NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallbacks for non-Gekko builds.
Declared asm functions with register parameters, e.g. register OSContext* fpuctx or ctx, to align parameter registers with the original PowerPC code.
Retained nofralloc on context/interrupt assembly routines that must not create a normal stack frame.
Replaced raw address-commented assembly with readable instructions but kept exact operations such as mfmsr/mtmsr, mfspr, mtfsf, lfd/stfd, psq_l/psq_st, and branch labels.
SetInterruptMask was translated into C using __cntlzw(mask) to select the highest-priority interrupt group, then writes subsystem MMIO registers for MEM, DSP, AI, EXI, and PI masks.

Some paired-single save/load accesses remained numeric offsets such as 456(fpuctx), 464(fpuctx), etc., suggesting exact addressing was kept where struct-field inline-asm syntax or matching constraints were less convenient.

review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether 0x80000000U should be abstracted as something like BASE_VIRT_ADDR.
Clarified that 0x80000000U in OS_INTRMASK_* macros is not an address but a mask value.
Asked how volatile should be handled across different platforms.
Responded that a special cross-platform volatile policy was probably unnecessary because volatile semantics are well-defined.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further rename lbl_804D738C or resolve remaining OSInterrupt sbss placeholder declarations.
Check follow-up work on non-MWERKS_GEKKO fallbacks in OSContext.c and OSInterrupt.c, since this PR used NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs.
Search for later discussion or changes around volatile MMIO declarations and interrupt globals.
Compare subsequent Dolphin OS decomp PRs that reuse OSInterruptMask or enum-derived mask macro patterns.
